options.A: Watchful waiting
options.B: Radical prostatectomy
options.C: Radiotherapy

question: Which of the following statement is true about hormonal treatment of prostatic cancer (PCa)?

options.A: Survival is improved if it is started for localized disease
options.B: There is no survival benefit if there is pelvic lymphadenopathy
options.C: PSA can be used to detect biochemical relapse
options.D: Anti-androgens are inferior to LHRH agonists in terms of survival

question: Which of the following is true about management of benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH)?

options.A: IPSS score can determine the surgical outcome
options.B: Urodynamic studies should be done in most patients
options.C: Quality of life score is an important tool for decision making
options.D: The global prostate size can predict severity of the problem

options.A: The kidneys
options.B: The adrenals

question: A 30-year-old male patient was admitted with Fournier’s gangrene for debridement. Which layers of testis & scrotum need to be debrided?

options.A: Scrotal skin only
options.B: Scrotal skin & Dartos layer
options.C: Scrotal skin, Dartos layer & external spermatic fascia
options.D: Scrotal skin, Dartos layer, external cremasteric & internal spermatic fascia

options.B: Hepatoma, pancreatic carcinoma, bronchogenic carcinoma

options.D: A & B

options.C: Endopelvic fascia
options.D: Puboprostatic fascia

question: A 6 year-old male child was admitted with suspected testicular torsion. Which of the following is true about surgical exploration?

options.A: It can be delayed till 24 hours and should be limited to affected side
options.B: It can be delayed but should include the asymptomatic side as well
options.C: It should be done immediately and should be limited to affected side
options.D: It should be done immediately and should also include asymptomatic side

options.A: It always results in significant symptoms
options.B: It is best diagnosed by endoscopy and urodynamic studies
options.C: It is easily diagnosed by symptoms of poor stream & straining
options.D: It always results in a residual urine volume of >100ml

options.A: Retinal angioma

question: Which of the following statement is false about surface anatomy of the genitourinary system?

options.A: The kidneys are approximately finger breadth superior to the iliac crest
options.B: Left renal hilum is at the level of transpyloric plane.
options.C: The ureters cross the tips of the transverse processes of the lumbar vertebrae.
options.D: The calyces, renal pelvis, ureters and bladder have different histology.

question: Which of the following statement is false about obstructive uropathy?

options.A: In unilateral obstruction renal blood flow and GFR increases in the first 1-2 hrs.
options.B: In bilateral ureteral obstruction ureteral pressure drops immediately.
options.C: In bilateral obstruction there is an increased production of prostaglandin and nitric oxide.
options.D: In bilateral obstruction there is an increased production of Atrial Natriuretic Peptide production.

question: Which of the following statement is true about diagnosis of obstructive uropahty?

options.A: Ultrasound has a high false positive rate in acute obstruction or dehydration.
options.B: CT KUB has limited ability to evaluate non- stone chronic obstructions
options.C: IVU is the preferred mode of investigation in the evaluation of the function of the kidneys.
options.D: Renal isotope scanning is the only modality providing dynamic renal function.

question: Which of the following statements is false about ureteric stones?

options.A: Mid ureteric ureteric stones are the most difficult to treat with hardest to treat with ESWL
options.B: Nifidipine can facilitate stone passage
options.C: Size of the stone is the most important predictor for spontaneous stone passage
options.D: None of the above

question: A 24-year-old gravida-II lady presented with urolithiasis. Which of the following statement is true?

options.A: Incidence is higher in pregnancy
options.B: Ureteroscopy is relatively safe
options.C: IVU is absolutely contraindicated
options.D: Treatment with ESWL is a relative contraindication

question: Which of the following statement is true about colorectal cancer?

options.A: Almost all arise from pre-existing adenoma
options.B: Lymph node involvement would make it Stage-III disease irrespective of T stage
options.C: Age is considered to be an important risk factor
options.D: Rectum and sigmoid colon are still the frequently affected parts

question: A 70-year-old male patient was found to have stage II mid sigmoid ca. What is the current recommended treatment?

options.A: Sigmoidectomy only
options.B: Sigmoidectomy followed by Chemotherapy
options.C: Noeadjuvant chemotherapy followed by sigmoidectomy
options.D: Left hemicolectomy

question: Which of the following statement is true about prognosis of colon and rectal cancer?

options.A: Rectal cancer has better prognosis than colon cancer because of its good response to chemoradiation
options.B: The prognosis is worse when lymph nodes are involved than mere adjacent organ involvement
options.C: The prognosis is similar in all age groups
options.D: Tumors related to LOH path way have better prognosis than those with microsatellite instability path way

question: Which of the following statements is/aretrue concerning the surgical management of colorectal cancer?

options.A: In general, the accepted safe margin to minimize anastomotic site recurrence in managing rectal cancer is 5 cm from the tumor margin
options.B: In handling mid rectal cancer, margins >2cm do not influence survival or local recurrence rates
options.C: Adequate lymphadenectomy (minimum of 12 LNs) plays important role in properly staging the tumor and effect cure
options.D: The length of bowel resected in colon cancer depends on which vessels are supplying the segment involved with the cancer.

question: Which of the following statements is false with regard to Ischemic colitis?

options.A: The splenic flexure and descending colon are the most vulnerable parts affected commonly
options.B: Typically, present with acute left iliac fossa pain followed by passage of loose bloody stool
options.C: Occlusion of the major mesenteric vessels is responsible for producing the ischemia in most cases
options.D: Only rarely does ischemic colitis progress to frank gangrene
options.E: Late stricture is one of the rare complication that may follow ischemic colitis

question: Which of the following is/are absolute indication for operative management of Diverticular disease?

options.A: Diverticulitis
options.B: Lower GI Bleeding
options.C: Stricture seen on Barium enema
options.D: Presence of persistent pain

question: A 40 year-old otherwise healthy female patient presented with sudden onset of severe abdominal cramp associated with frequent vomiting and inability to pass flatus and stool of 6 hours duration. She was screaming intermittently and her BP was 80/50, PR 104/minute. Abdomen was moderately distended and tender all over. What is the most likely diagnosis?

options.A: Ileo-colic intussusception
options.B: Ileosigmoid knotting
options.C: Mesenteric thrombosis of the Small bowel
options.D: Perforated PUD

question: Which of the following is false regarding anal carcinomas?

options.A: Most anal carcinomas are the epidermoid type
options.B: Wide local excision can achieve 100% local control for T1 (<2cm) tumor
options.C: Chemoradiation is currently the first line treatment modality for anal cancer
options.D: Recurrence is most likely to occur within 12 months of the initial treatment
options.E: Salvage anorectal excision for recurrent disease achieves cure rates of 50%.

question: Which of the following statement is true about anal fissure?

options.A: It is located just above the dentate line
options.B: It is located at the posterior mid line in more than 90% of patients
options.C: Surgery of choice for a midline fissure is excision of the fissure and posterior internal sphincterotomy
options.D: Lateral partial subcutaneous sphincterotomy is the operation of choice for nonmidline fissures
options.E: Laterally located fissures have better prognosis

question: Which of the following statement is true about fistula in ano?

options.A: Fistulography is very helpful to differentiate between low and high fistula in ano
options.B: The most common type of fistula is intersphincteric
options.C: Opening the entire fistulous tract is necessary to achieve cure
options.D: Seton application achieves 80% cure in high fistulas

question: Which of the following is not among recognized complication of open AAA repair?

options.A: Ischemic colitis
options.B: Paraplegia
options.C: Declamping shock
options.D: Phlegmatica cerulean dolens

question: Which of the following is not an indication to repair AAA?

options.A: Diameter >5.5cm

options.C: Aneurysm in females irrespective of the size
options.D: Symptomatic aneurysm

question: Which of the following is true about occlusive disease of the femoral artery?

options.B: Small bowel atresia
options.C: Malroatation

options.A: Rectum

options.C: Transverse colon
options.D: Cecum

question: A 2-year-old has a right side impalpable testis but normal left testis. Which of the following statement is true?

options.A: There is greater than 90% chance of finding the testis and or cord structures at right inguinal exploration
options.B: There is greater than 10% chance of spontaneous decent of right testis in 12 months
options.C: Orchidopexy will significantly improves the function of the right testis
options.D: Orchidopexy will reduce the frequency of malignancy occurring in the right testis

options.A: U/S of the cyst

question: Which of the following roughly corresponds to the 24hours maintenance fluid, electrolyte and dextrose requirement of 10kg infant?

options.A: 1000ml water; 30meq of Na+; 30meq of K+; 30mmeq of Cl- and 50gm of dextrose
options.B: 1000ml of water; 60meq of Na+; 20meq of K+; 30mmeq of Cl- and 500gm of dextrose

options.C: Treat the patient with anti-inflammatory medication and scrotal elevation

question: A 6-hour male neonate was born with absent anal opening. Which of the following is true?

options.A: Urgent colostomy should be done right away
options.B: Anoplasy without colostomy can be done if the anomaly is ARM with rectovesical fistula
options.C: Continence after surgery is almost nil
options.D: A divided descending colostomy is ideal for the management of anorectal malformations

question: A 14-year-old boy presents with a 4-hour history of acute right testicular pain. It was associated with nausea and vomiting. On physical exam, the right testis is very tender to palpation and is high-riding. The cremasteric reflex is absent on the right and present on the left. Whichof the following is appropriate?

options.A: Take the patient immediately to the operating room for scrotal exploration and possible bilateral orchidopexy
options.B: Perform a testicular ultrasonography to evaluate for the presence of blood flow
options.C: Treat the patient with anti-inflammatory medication and scrotal elevation
options.D: Observe the patient to see if the pain improves with intravenous narcotics

question: A 2-year-old female child presented with a huge left sided renal mass. Intraoperatively, there was tumor rupture. Which of the following statement is true?

options.A: Preoperative chemotherapy increases the risk of tumor rupture with manipulation
options.B: Rupture of the renal capsule or presence of tumor spillage will upgrade the tumor stage
options.C: Gerota’s fascia should not be touched order decrease the chances of rupture
options.D: Peritoneal soiling is currently considered a stage 2, which adds radiation and chemotherapy to the management

question: A 21-day-old male neonate presented with a compliant of persistent projectile non-bilious vomiting after each feed and hungry after each feed, physical examination revealed an olive shaped mass. Up on admission, Serum K level was 2.9. Which of the following is true regarding the management of this patient?

options.A: Serum carbon dioxide (<30 mmol/L), chloride (>100 mmol/L), and potassium (4.5-6.5 mmol/L) levels as markers of resuscitation.
options.B: Postoperative emesis on the third operative day should raise a suspicion of incomplete myotomy and barium meal examination has to be ordered
options.C: Postoperative feeding should be started after 12 hours
options.D: On abdominal ultrasound pyloric channel length of 12 mm is diagnostic

question: Which of the following microorganism can lead to emphysematous cholecystitis

options.A: E. coli
options.B: Bacteroids spp
options.C: Clostridiums perfereiges
options.D: Klebsella spp

question: The Tokyo guidelines severity grading for Acute Cholecystitis is sometimes used for acute cholecystitis. Which of the following Is correct?

options.A: Elevated WBC more than 18000 will be Grade 3
options.B: Marked local inflammation with Biliary Peritonitis will make Grade 3
options.C: Duration of symptoms more than 72 Hrs will Be Grade 1
options.D: Palpable Tender GB will make Grade 2

question: Which of the following features are against the possibility of primary Sclorresing Cholangitis

options.A: The Presence of Periductal Fibrosis
options.B: The Presence of multiple Ductal Dilatations
options.C: Findings of Multiple Stricture
options.D: The majority are related to IgG4 associated Chalangiopathy and finding of this would suggest PSC.
options.E: None of is suggestive of against the possibility Of PSC.

question: Which of the following patient does not need post mastectomy radiotherapy

options.A: 3cm sized bilateral breast cancer
options.B: Multiple matted axillary LNs fixed to the chest wall
options.C: 3cm breast cancer with postmastectomy of 6 lymph nodes positive for cancer
options.D: 2cm tumor fixed to the chest wall
